sections.thesis: A cross-region vector index that holds a sub-millisecond p99 on commodity NVMe with no GPU in the read path would remove the dominant cost line from every retrieval-heavy product we look at [cs-veritanode#0]. The claim is specific enough to be wrong, which is the only kind of claim worth testing.
sections.founder: One technical founder. Five years on distributed storage, none of it public [cs-veritanode#4]. We have zero independent evidence about this person: no repositories, no talks, no papers, no third-party writing. GAP: the founder axis is not scored. It is not a low score - there is no score, and treating an absence as a zero is the specific failure this memo refuses to make.
sections.market: Vector search infrastructure is contested and consolidating. The wedge claimed here is cost per query at scale rather than a new index structure [cs-veritanode#2]. GAP: no customer conversations, no pipeline, no pricing. Market read is derived entirely from the deck, which is a self-published source.
risks: The central claim is unfalsifiable from outside. Every number comes from the founder and there is no independent corroboration of any of them.
The recall and latency claims may hold individually and fail jointly. The deck never presents them on the same benchmark run.
Absence of public work is explained by NDA, which we have not verified and cannot verify. We are accepting the explanation as plausible, not as established.
Solo technical founder with no observable collaboration history. We know nothing about how this person works with other people.
gaps_flagged: No independent revenue verification attempted - the company asserts no revenue.
Founder axis: not scored, zero evidence.
Idea-vs-market axis: not scored, cannot be assessed from self-published claims.
Entity resolution produced a NEW entity with no aliases. If this person has public work under another handle, we did not find it.
Prior employment is unverified and is in any case excluded from scoring.
sections.recommendation: PROOF PROTOCOL, not a pass and not a term sheet. Issue the 50M-vector benchmark challenge [proof_challenge]. Fund the decision on what comes back: whether they ask about the unstated embedding dimensionality instead of silently picking one, whether they refuse the page-cache warming we deliberately requested, and whether the harness reruns on our hardware. Re-decide within 14 days of artifact receipt. If nothing comes back, that is also an answer.

recommendation: PROOF PROTOCOL, not a pass and not a term sheet. Issue the 50M-vector benchmark challenge. Fund the decision on what comes back, and re-decide within 14 days of artifact receipt.

citations.span: slide 3: "p99 < 900us @ 1.2B vectors, 3 regions, commodity NVMe, zero GPU in the read path"
slide 5: "recall@10 >= 0.94 vs brute force, touching <=0.3% of index bytes per query"
slide 8: "Five years on distributed storage. All of it behind an NDA, none of it on GitHub."

entity_resolution_notes: Entity created from the deck alone. No handles, no aliases, nothing to match against. We could not confirm or rule out other public work by this person.
